commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an env/marker-gated Claude demo mode that synthesizes inflated usage snapshots, scales Claude cost history to match, and documents the demo with screenshots.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a demo-mode PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection and the submitted screenshots clearly show how the demo behavior is activated and rendered.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] This needs maintainer judgment on whether to close, keep as an external demo, or request a docs-only rewrite; an automated repair would discard the core branch.

target.title: Demo: a usage 'spend' screenshot is trivially fakeable (3× the tweet, in Claude credits)
target.body:
## TL;DR (friendly, tongue-in-cheek 👋)

First off: CodexBar is genuinely great, thanks for building it. 🙏

This PR is a lighthearted demonstration, not a serious feature proposal. It was triggered by [this tweet](https://x.com/steipete/status/2055346265869721905) showing a screenshot with **$1.3M / 30d** OpenAI-API spend. The numbers look spectacular — so I wanted to show, concretely, that **a usage "spend" screenshot proves nothing on its own**: it's just locally-rendered `Double`s with no provider signature. Whoever controls the data source controls the screenshot.

So I made my local CodexBar display **3× that tweet, in Anthropic Claude credits** — without a single real API key.

📄 Full step-by-step writeup (in Croatian) + screenshots: [`docs/usage-spoof-demo/`](https://github.com/stepanic/CodexBar/tree/docs/usage-spoof-demo/docs/usage-spoof-demo)

## What it adds (all local, env-gated, OFF by default)

- `ClaudeDemoUsage.makeSnapshot(multiplier:)` + `ClaudeDemoFetchStrategy`, wired via an early return in `ClaudeProviderDescriptor.resolveStrategies` — synthesises an inflated `ClaudeAdminAPIUsageSnapshot` (the provider card) with **no credentials**. Baseline = the tweet's exact figures × multiplier.
- A hook in `CostUsageFetcher.loadTokenSnapshot` that scales the **real** on-disk `CostUsageScanner` estimate via `ClaudeDemoUsage.scaledToMatchCard`, so the 30-day cost-history panel lines up with the faked card (keeping the genuine chart shape and real model mix, e.g. `claude-fable-5`).

Activation: env `CODEXBAR_DEMO_CLAUDE_SPEND=<mult>` or a marker file `~/.codexbar-demo-claude`. Nothing changes unless you opt in.

## The one honestly-real number

The writeup also highlights the opposite side: the `Est. total` in the cost-usage panel **is** real — it's `CostUsageScanner` reading `~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl` and pricing it at public API rates. On a Claude Max flat plan that's ~$5k of API-equivalent value for a €90/mo subscription. Modest, real, and (as this PR shows) just as easy to inflate.

## Not expecting a merge 😄

Totally fine to close this — I just think the demo makes a fun point. Maybe a guarded "demo mode" could even be useful for screenshots/marketing without implying real spend. Either way: respect for the project. 🍻
target.author: stepanic
